@graph.name: Mercedes-Benz models fitted with the M276/M278 family

mainEntity.name: How much does an M276/M278 engine replacement cost?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned M276/M278 typically costs £3,500–£6,500 supply only [ESTIMATED], varying by V6 or V8 spec and whether turbos are included. Fitting adds £780–£1,300 depending on labour rates and model.

mainEntity.name: Is the M276/M278 a reliable engine?

acceptedAnswer.text: The M276/M278 rate 4.0★ out of 5 based on specialist feedback and UK owner data [MB-INTERVIEW]. They are refined and generally robust, but timing chain wear and oil leaks are the main areas to monitor.

mainEntity.name: What is the most common M276/M278 failure?

acceptedAnswer.text: Timing chain stretch is the most common reported issue, particularly on higher-mileage M276 V6s. Oil leaks from the V‑engine valley and valve cover gaskets are also frequent as the engines age.

mainEntity.name: Which Mercedes models use the M276/M278?

acceptedAnswer.text: The M276/M278 powers E‑Class, S‑Class, CLS, ML/GLE, GLS, GL, SL, G‑Class, and some C‑Class and AMG models — covering a wide range from the early 2010s to around 2020.

mainEntity.name: How long does the M276/M278 last?

acceptedAnswer.text: With regular oil changes and proactive timing chain inspection, the M276/M278 routinely exceeds 150,000 miles. Many specialists report V8 M278 examples at 200,000+ with proper maintenance [MB-INTERVIEW].

hero.h1: Mercedes-Benz M276/M278 Engine — Specs, Reliability & Replacement Cost
hero.subHeadline: The Mercedes M276 is a 3.5L V6 (or 4.7L V8 twin-turbo M278) — silky smooth with strong torque, but timing chain wear and oil leaks are notable. Backed by real UK enquiries in 2025 [MB-INTERVIEW].

verdictRating.confidence: 88% confidence — based on UK specialist feedback and owner-reported data across a broad range of models.

rows.score: 12 (Mix of well-understood repairs (timing chain, oil leaks) and V-engine complexity)
16 (Good parts availability from Mercedes and aftermarket; 4.7L V8 has strong support)
16 (Reconditioned cost 30–44% of lowest host vehicle value)

verdictRating.oneLineVerdict: The M276/M278 are refined, torquey V6/V8s — timing chain stretch and oil leaks are the main watch-points — buy with full service history and inspect chain health.
verdictRating.bestFor: Buyers wanting a smooth, powerful petrol Mercedes with modern features.
verdictRating.avoidIf: You're not prepared for potential timing chain or V-engine oil leak costs.

costGuide.labourEstimate: Fitting an M276/M278 typically takes 12–16 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent) or £110–£160/hr (London/SE dealer) [ESTIMATED].
